patterns: minimal-ai: use deterministic logic first, LLMs only when reasoning or generation is required
structured-outputs: always request structured (JSON) outputs from LLMs, validate with Pydantic
chain-of-responsibility: break complex tasks into sequential steps, each independently testable
cognitive-architecture: design data flow diagrams before writing AI code
guardrails: prompt injection detection, PII filtering, output validation on every LLM call
fallback-chains: if primary model fails, fall back to secondary, then to deterministic response
structured-citations: every AI-generated answer includes file name, quote, and line number
multi-stage-pipeline: decouple ingestion → transformation → agent processing → delivery as independent stages
specialized-agents: one agent per task (summarizer, ranker, curator) — never one monolithic agent
lightweight-transformation: prefer lightweight tools over heavy ML models for content extraction
mcp-first: expose functions as MCP tools for composable capabilities
tool-chaining: design MCP servers as composable units chained via a single prompt
principles: The AI layer is a thin wrapper — most of the system is regular software engineering.
Every LLM call is traced (input, output, latency, token cost) via Langfuse.
Never expose raw LLM output to users without validation and sanitization.
Embeddings are generated once at ingestion time, not at query time (except for the query itself).
Hybrid retrieval is mandatory — BM25 + dense vectors + RRF + cross-encoder reranking.
Provider SDKs are isolated behind a service interface — switching providers is a config change.
Context windows are finite — chunk documents intelligently, don't stuff everything in.
Start with semantic hybrid RAG — upgrade to agentic when query complexity demands it.
Agent tools return errors as messages, never raise exceptions.
Enforce hard limits on agent tool calls and file read sizes.

tiered_loading: level-0 (abstract): one-line summary per area — scan entire system in under 2,000 tokens
level-1 (overview): short description of purpose, workflows, inter-area relationships
level-2 (full): deep data accessed only when agent confirms relevance
context_hub_structure: identity: mission, values — loaded into every system prompt
inbox: temporary staging for unprocessed ideas
knowledge: permanent reference library (SOPs, docs, domain guides)
archive: stale data — agents explicitly ignore this
principles: Context is King — output quality is determined by context quality, not the model.
Separate structured data (PostgreSQL) from unstructured context (Markdown/Git).
Agent skills are version-controlled Markdown — installable, removable, auditable.
Load minimum context needed to decide if more is required. Never front-load full documents.

system_prompt_calibration.too_specific: every edge case hardcoded → context bloat, model ignores core rules
system_prompt_calibration.sweet_spot: structured behavioral constraints with positive few-shot examples
system_prompt_calibration.principle: LLMs are poor at negative constraints ('Don't do X'). Provide a picture of success instead.

modular_architecture.strict_workflows: deterministic routing — for automations without human in the loop
modular_architecture.autonomous_agents: tools in a loop — for human-in-the-loop scenarios. Avoid for backend tasks without guards.
tool_engineering: concise-manifests: describe 'what' and 'when' to call — not implementation details
sub-agent-delegation: router to specialized sub-agents with limited toolsets

dynamic_context_management.state_machine_injection: Track state in database, pull only relevant prompt fragments at runtime.
memory_management: conversation-pruning: replace old messages with state summaries
sliding-window: last N turns + compressed summary of everything before
selective-injection: only inject data relevant to the current reasoning step

failure_analysis: 1. analyze-trace: see what the model actually 'saw' at the moment of failure
2. check-context: too much noise? too little signal? outdated information?
3. identify-source: RAG retrieval? tool description? conversation history? system prompt?
4. inject-positive-examples: only after trace analysis should you refine the prompt

stages: 1. ingestion: consolidate heterogeneous sources into common schema — low-latency strategies
2. transformation: convert raw data to LLM-ready format — lightweight tools
3. agent-processing: specialized agents per task (summarizer, ranker, curator, classifier)
4. persistence: store outputs with state flags (processed_at, delivered_at) — idempotent reruns
5. delivery: automated distribution (email, webhooks, APIs) — close the loop
design_principles: decoupled-stages: ingestion failure must not impede processing of cached data
common-schema: all sources normalized before agent processing
deterministic-over-generative: reserve LLM calls for tasks requiring reasoning

deep_dive_files.rag: .claude/stack/ai-rag.json — load when working on retrieval, embeddings, or search
deep_dive_files.evaluation: .claude/stack/ai-eval.json — load when working on LLM testing, judge prompts, or alignment

key_libraries: langchain: LLM orchestration, chains, document loaders, text splitters
langgraph: stateful agent workflows with cycles and branching
langfuse: LLM observability, tracing, prompt management
tiktoken: token counting for context window management
bm25s: sparse retrieval with BM25 scoring (Lucene method)
numpy: high-performance vector operations, index persistence (.npy)
pydantic-ai: tool-call orchestration with strict data validation
ripgrep: Rust-based high-performance search for agentic RAG
